LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING: NO ARGUMENT. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING: DEFENDANTS SARIT SIMAYOF, ADI SIMAYOF AND WORLD OF CHARMS INC.'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS IS OVERRULED AS TO THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION FOR BREACH OF CONTRACT. PLAINTIFF FILED A NOTICE OF ERRATA ATTACHING EXHIBIT A TO THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT ON SEPTEMBER 6, 2012, THUS RENDERING MOOT DEFENDANTS' ARGUMENT ON UNCERTAINTY AND INABILITY TO ASCERTAIN THE NATURE OF THE AGREEMENT. DEFENDANTS' DEMURRER TO THE FIFTH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR FRAUD IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND IN TEN DAYS. THE FACTS SET FORTH IN THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT DO NOT SATISFY THE STRICT PLEADING STANDARDS REQUIRED FOR A FRAUD CAUSE OF ACTION, AMONG OTHER THINGS FAILING TO SPECIFY THE AUTHOR AND RECIPIENT OF THE STATEMENTS, THE MEANS BY WHICH THEY WERE MADE, AND THEIR DATE. E.G., LAZAR V. SUPERIOR COURT, 12 CAL.4TH 631, 645 (1996). ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: CURTIS E. A. KARNOW, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, NOT REPORTED.

CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Nov-06-2024 Off Calendar

Motion To Enforce Settlements And Enter Judgment Pursuant To Stipulations
Off Calendar Nov-22-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Daniel S. Frankston, Esq., T:415.202.1880, for plaintiff/moving party;
Lorna Walker, Esq. of the L/O of Lorna Walker, T:415.334.1600, for plaintiff/moving party;
Sarit Simhayoff Cohen, Esq. of the L/O of Sarit Simhayoff Cohen, T:415.407.4197, for defendant/opposing party.

RULING: The matter was argued. At the conclusion of arguments, the Court adopted its tentative ruling on PLAINTIFF LISA EITANI's Motion To Enforce Settlements And Enter Judgment Pursuant To Stipulations.

Plaintiff's motion for entry of judgment is granted. (See Code Civ. Proc., 664.6.) Plaintiff is entitled to a judgment of $456,500 against Adi Simayof, Simayof, Inc., a California corporation, and SIMUS, Inc., a California corporation, dba Simayof; and to a judgment of $135,000 against World of Charms, Inc., a Nevada corporation, aka WOC, Inc., aka Sarit Simayof Reveal Your Charm and Sarit Simayof, aka Sarit Simhayoff-Cohen, an individual (hereinafter "WOC").

The evidence indicates that Adi Simayof breached the stipulation for entry of judgment by failing to make timely payments. Mr. Simayof failed to provide Plaintiff with sufficient financial information to warrant a modification of the payment plan to $500 per month. (E.g., Frankston Decl. 4, 6, 8, 9, 10-16, 18; Frankston Reply Decl. 4, 5.) Evidence further shows that Mr. Simayof did not properly document all of the funds he was receiving. (E.g., Frankston Decl., Ex. 34.)

Entry of judgment against WOC is appropriate because Mr. Simyof breached the stipulation and WOC agreed to guarantee $135,000 in payments. The stipulation that WOC executed constitutes a stipulation for entry of judgment per Code of Civil Procedure, 664.6. WOC's argument that it never received a notice of default fails. Plaintiff sent a notice of default to Ms. Simhayoff-Cohen on 7/9/15 and 2/17/17. (Walker Dec., Exs. D and G.) While the letters are not expressly addressed to World of Charms, Inc., Ms. Simhayoff-Cohen is an officer of that entity that signed the stipulation and she is also its attorney.

Order and Judgments signed in open court.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Maria A. Torreano CSR#8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Enforce Settlements And Enter Judgments Pursuant To Stipulations
Off Calendar Apr-25-2017 Unknown
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. LORNA WALKER, TEL. NO. 1-415-334-1600 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ADI SIMAYOF, TEL. NO. 1-415-699-5097, DEFENDANT APPEARING IN PRO PER;
SARIT SIMHAYOFF-COHEN, TEL. NO. 1-415-407-4197 DEFENDANT APPEARING IN PRO PER.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Plaintiff Eitani's motion to enforce settlements and enter judgments pursuant to stipulation is denied without prejudice because Ms. Eitani fails to show that she negotiated Mr. Simayof's request for a payment modification in good faith. "[W]here a contract confers on one party a discretionary power affecting the rights of the other, a duty is imposed to exercise that discretion in good faith and in accordance with fair dealing." Peak-Las Positas Partners v. Bollag (2009) 172 Cal.App.4th 101, 106, quoting Locke v. Warner Bros. (1997) 57 Cal.App.4th 354, 363.) The court applies an objective test regarding the exercise of plaintiff's discretion because finances are involved. (Id.) Assuming that Ms. Eitani's request for a security was permissible under the Simayof stipulation, it was unreasonable as a matter of law in the instant circumstances. Ms. Eitani insisted on a security interest on Mr. Simayof's sister's house (Walker Dec., par. 16; Simayof Dec., par. 31.) Given Mr. Simayof's lack of interest in that property, it would be unfeasible for him to provide the requested security to Ms. Eitani. The court denies the motion as to the World of Charm defendants because Ms. Eitani fails to show that she complied with her obligation to negotiate with Mr. Simayof in good faith. The court rejects the new arguments that Ms. Eitani raises in the reply brief, e.g., Mr. Simayof did not meet the requirements to even request a modification or that he did not proffer sufficient evidence. Order signed in open court. Judge: Stephen Murphy; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Carol Karen CSR #8189 =302/SM

Off Calendar Sep-19-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: GARY BOSCH, ESQ., LAW OFFICE OF GARY BOSCH, 415.345.4100, FOR DEFENDANTs SARIT SIMAYOF, ADI SIMAYOF AND WORLD OF CHARMS INC.
DAVID MARCOLINI, ESQ., 415.568.0742, SPECIALLY APPEARING FOR PLAINTIFF LISA KROGER EITANI, INDIVIDUALLY AND DBA KROGER GEMS.

RULING: NO ARGUMENT. The Court adopted its tentative ruling: DEFENDANTs SARIT SIMAYOF, ADI SIMAYOF AND WORLD OF CHARMS INC.'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS is overruled as to the first cause of action for breach of contract. Plaintiff filed a Notice of Errata attaching Exhibit A to the First Amended Complaint on September 6, 2012, thus rendering moot Defendants' argument on uncertainty and inability to ascertain the nature of the agreement. Defendants' demurrer to the fifth cause of action for fraud is sustained with leave to amend in ten days. The facts set forth in the First Amended Complaint do not satisfy the strict pleading standards required for a fraud cause of action, among other things failing to specify the author and recipient of the statements, the means by which they were made, and their date. E.g., Lazar v. Superior Court, 12 Cal.4th 631, 645 (1996). Order signed in open Court. Judge: Curtis E. A. Karnow, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Not reported.
